message.content:
I'll analyze this UI screenshot in detail to help you rebuild it exactly in Flutter. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

## 1. Exact Text Content

**Header:**
- "Waterfall Design" (top left)
- "Project Dashboard" (smaller text below "Waterfall Design")
- User icon with "User" text in top right corner

**Sidebar:**
- "Project Dashboard" (header)
- "Home" (active/highlighted item)
- "Project Overview"
- "Team Members"
- "Timeline"
- "Resources"
- "Design Management" (active/highlighted section)
- "Design Documents"
- "Design Tools"
- "System Architecture"
- "Collaborators"
- "Settings"

**Main Content:**

**Progress Section:**
- "0%" (large red text)
- "Early Stages" (smaller text below)
- Red warning icon in top right of this section

**Notes Section:**
- "Notes" (section header)
- "Summarize design goals, decisions, and key considerations" (description)
- Formatting toolbar with: B, I, U, T, and other icons

**Design Management Section:**
- "Design Management" (section header)
- "Design Strategy & Governance" (subsection)
- Dropdown labels: "Methodology", "Tools", "Resources"
- "Design Documents" (card header with green document icon)
- "Design Tools" (card header with yellow tools icon)
- "System Architecture" (section header)
- "Design Tools" (subsection)
- "Rich Text Editor" (tool name)
- "Design Notes" (section header)
- "Start drafting your design narrative here. Use the toolbar above for quick formatting." (placeholder text)

## 2. Sidebar Items

The sidebar contains the following navigation items with "Home" and "Design Management" highlighted/active:
- Project Dashboard (header)
- Home (active)
- Project Overview
- Team Members
- Timeline
- Resources
- Design Management (active section)
  - Design Documents
  - Design Tools
  - System Architecture
  - Collaborators
- Settings

## 3. Header/Appbar

- Left side: "Waterfall Design" (main title) with "Project Dashboard" (subtitle)
- Right side: User profile icon with "User" text

## 4. Main Content Layout

The main content area uses a single column scroll layout with multiple sections stacked vertically.

## 5. Cards/Sections

**Progress Card:**
- Large "0%" in red
- "Early Stages" text
- Red warning icon
- Pink/red background area below

**Notes Card:**
- "Notes" header
- Description text
- Text formatting toolbar
- Large text area

**Design Management Section:**
- "Design Management" header
- "Design Strategy & Governance" subsection with three dropdowns

**Two-Column Cards:**
- Left: "Design Documents" card with green document icon, "No documents added" text, and green "Add Document" button
- Right: "Design Tools" card with yellow tools icon, "No tools configured" text, and yellow "Add Tool" button

**System Architecture Section:**
- "System Architecture" header
- "Live demo" link
- "Design Tools" subsection showing "Rich Text Editor"

**Design Notes Section:**
- "Design Notes" header
- Large text area with placeholder text
- Formatting toolbar

## 6. Design Strategy & Governance Dropdowns

Three dropdowns with labels:
- "Methodology"
- "Tools" 
- "Resources"

## 7. Two-Column Card Section

**Design Documents Card:**
- Green document icon
- "Design Documents" title
- "No documents added" text
- Green "Add Document" button

**Design Tools Card:**
- Yellow tools icon
- "Design Tools" title
- "No tools configured" text
- Yellow "Add Tool" button

## 8. System Architecture Section

Contains:
- "System Architecture" header
- "Live demo" link
- "Design Tools" subsection
- "Rich Text Editor" tool listed

## 9. Design Tools Section

Shows:
- "Rich Text Editor" as the only tool

## 10. Progress Indicators

- Large "0%" in red text indicating progress
- Red warning icon

## 11. Button Colors

- Green for "Add Document" button
- Yellow/amber for "Add Tool" button

## 12. Notes Section Formatting Toolbar

Icons visible: Bold (B), Italic (I), Underline (U), and other text formatting tools

## 13. Live Demo Link

"Live demo" text appears in the System Architecture section

## Additional Details

- The overall color scheme is light/white with colored accents (red for warnings, green for documents, yellow for tools)
- The layout is clean and minimal with plenty of white space
- Cards have subtle borders and shadows
- The active sidebar item has a yellow highlight
- The "Design Management" section in the sidebar is expanded showing its sub-items

This detailed analysis should help you recreate the exact UI in Flutter with all the specific text, colors, and layout elements.
